summary: Verified the NEW band strategy on /s/passa-a-fibra: titleband is always a uniform white→gray-100 linear gradient with slate-900 black text, fully independent of hero_image. Tested with 3 distinct hero images (WindTre cloudinary, orange Unsplash camera, purple plasma) plus empty hero — band computed style is byte-identical in every case: backgroundImage='linear-gradient(rgb(255, 255, 255) 0%, rgb(243, 244, 246) 100%)' and color='rgb(15, 23, 42)'. Source confirmed clean: no sampleHeroColor, no transition-[background]. Contrast title-on-white = 17.85:1 (WCAG AAA pass). Cleanup performed (hero_image reset to '').
critical_code_review_comments: StoreLanding.js lines 49-58: bandRgb and bandRgbDark are top-level consts (not state) — correct since they never change. Inline literal constants would be marginally cleaner but the named consts improve readability.

Line 287-289 comment block still says 'dynamic gradient sampled from the image bottom' — this is stale (sampling was removed). Should be updated to reflect the new uniform-white behavior. Minor doc drift, no functional impact.

Line 271-278 : crossOrigin='anonymous' is now vestigial (it was only needed for canvas pixel sampling). Safe to remove since no canvas reads hero pixels anymore. Not blocking — leaving it doesn't break anything.
test_credentials: admin@example.com / admin123 (used to PATCH /api/stores/{id}/landings/{id} for hero swap during testing)
seed_data_creation: None permanent. Temporarily PATCHed hero_image to orange (unsplash 1502920917128) then purple plasma (unsplash 1530973428) then reset to '' as final cleanup. Variant 8f67a135-1d80-49d3-87e3-f8ea5ba90d0d hero_image is now empty.
context_for_next_testing_agent: URL: https://qr-deploy-1.preview.emergentagent.com/s/passa-a-fibra. Store id 6a383aa59bbc459260a5f6e7, variant id 8f67a135-1d80-49d3-87e3-f8ea5ba90d0d. The band [data-testid=store-landing-titleband] is now STATIC: backgroundImage 'linear-gradient(rgb(255,255,255) 0%, rgb(243,244,246) 100%)' and color 'rgb(15,23,42)' regardless of hero. Note: getComputedStyle normalizes 'linear-gradient(180deg, ...)' by dropping the '180deg,' because 180deg = default 'to bottom' direction — this is browser-normalized output, not a code issue. Subtitle uses text-slate-700 (rgb(51,65,85)). Cleanup: hero_image is currently '' on the variant; main agent should re-set it if needed for demo.
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration verifies an intentional product pivot (drop image color sampling, use uniform white band always). All acceptance criteria met.
